confirmations.reason: 'Strict Mode' restricts the agent's access to external resources and sensitive operations — this is an access-control/sandboxing mechanism. Prompt-injection protection specifically means defending against malicious instructions embedded in tool outputs or web content. Restricting access is adjacent but does not directly detect or neutralize injected instructions.

A 'Fine-Grained Permissions Engine' governs what operations the agent is allowed to perform (access control / authorization). Security scanning requires actively inspecting code or agent actions against a threat model for vulnerabilities. Permissions enforcement and threat-model scanning are distinct; the former prevents unauthorized actions but does not scan for security issues.

'Artifact Review Policy' controls how a human user reviews and approves agent-proposed plans/artifacts before changes land — this is a human-approval gate, not an automated agent review of diffs/PRs for issues. The capability requires the agent to perform the review; this feature puts a human in the loop instead.

'Verification loops' describe the agent running local test commands (unit tests, build scripts) after making changes and iterating — this is an agentic self-correction loop during task execution. Evals/testing harness means running structured evaluations against the agent's own behavior (benchmarks, regression suites). These are related but distinct: one is in-task iteration, the other is systematic behavioral evaluation of the agent itself.

'Scheduled Tasks' let users schedule time-based messages to agents while away. Remote control means actively steering, monitoring, or approving agent runs from a remote client (mobile/web) in real time. Scheduling is a fire-and-forget trigger mechanism, not an interactive remote control surface.

'Scheduled Tasks' is about sending pre-configured messages to agents on a time-based schedule. Notifications means the system proactively pushing updates (progress, completion alerts) to the user. These are opposite directions of communication: scheduled tasks push from user to agent on a timer, while notifications push from agent/system to user.
